THE COURT MAINTAINS JURISDICTION TO RULE ON THE VEXATIOUS LITIGANT MOTION DESPITE THE DISMISSAL OF THE CASE PER PITTMAN V. BECK PARK APARTMENTS LTD. (2018) 20 CAL.APP. 5TH 1009, 1021. THE COURT NOTES THAT IN AT LEAST TWO PRIOR LAW SUITS, VIVIEN MOHANNA HAS APPEARED IN PRO PER WITH POWER OF ATTORNEY IN CONTRAVENTION OF LAW. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, REPORTED (D501)

Off Calendar May-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Richard Antognini, Esq., 916-295-4896, appearing for Keyhan Mohanna, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Josh Valene, Esq. of Dinsmore & Sandelmann LLP, 310-318-1220, appearing for Pamela Jackson, for Defendant and moving party.

Court reporter: Vicky Gross CSR #5525, telephone: (415)551-3730; email: vgross@sftc.org

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) .

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PAMELA JACKSON's Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16.Complaint is MOOT. A dismissal was filed by Plaintiff on May 8, 2019. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To 1)Deem Plaintiff Keyhan Mohanna A Vexatious Litigant, 2)Enter A Pre-Filing Order Pursuant To C.C.P. 391.7, And 3)Dismiss The Present Case And-Or Order That Security Be Posted For Benefit Of De
Off Calendar May-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Richard Antognini, Esq., 916-295-4896, appearing for Keyhan Mohanna, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Josh Valene, Esq. of Dinsmore & Sandelmann LLP, 310-318-1220, appearing for Pamela Jackson, for Defendant and moving party.

Court reporter: Vicky Gross CSR #5525, telephone: (415)551-3730; email: vgross@sftc.org

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) .

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PAMELA JACKSON's Motion To 1)Deem Plaintiff Keyhan Mohanna A Vexatious Litigant, 2)Enter A Pre-Filing Order Pursuant To C.C.P. 391.7, And 3)Dismiss The Present Case And-Or Order That Security Be Posted For Benefit Of Defendant is DENIED. The Court maintains jurisdiction to rule on the vexatious litigant motion despite the dismissal of the case per Pittman v. Beck Park Apartments Ltd. (2018) 20 Cal.App. 5th 1009, 1021. The Court notes that in at least two prior law suits, Vivien Mohanna has appeared in pro per with power of attorney in contravention of law. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, reported (D501)

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.)

Compliance with Tentative Ruling Rule.
